parameters.text:
Analyze image and then return in JSON Response that has the only following Value:
Status, From, To, Date, Amount

parameters.content:
## Extract text from image
**Prompt for Gemini**
Analyze image and then return in JSON Response that has the only following value: Status, From, To, Date, Amount

## Gemini AI Assistant

AI Assistant using Gemini 2.0 Flash Experiment unlocks new possibilities for AI agents - intelligent systems that can use memory, reasoning, and planning to complete tasks for you.

## Reply to User

Reply the processing result to the user without coding or OCR processing.

## Insert result to Google Sheet
Get all important information from the Pay Slip and insert into Google Sheet in the same format that we have provided in our prompt.

## Get Line Message from User
User can send message in both text and Pay Slip image then classify the message type in text or image so we could have single workflow for AI Assistant that support anything.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details
google Palm  A P I details
configurationSteps: Locate the webhook node named "Line: Messaging API".
Copy the Test Webhook URL provided by n8n.
Configure your source application (e.g. Stripe, Typeform) to send event payloads to this URL.
Open the "Window Buffer Memory" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Line: Get Image" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Message Classification" node (n8n-nodes-base.switch) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Text Message Processing" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Image Message Processing" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Line: Response to User" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Line: Text Response to User" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Text from Slip Result" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Gemini for Text" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Gemini for Image" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Message Type".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
